title: Serverless MySQL (PlanetScale)
description: PlanetScale is a serverless MySQL-compatible database powered by Vitess. Key features: non-blocking schema changes (no downtime migrations), database branching, horizontal sharding, and deploy requests for schema migrations. Ideal for teams needing MySQL compatibility with cloud-native scalability and safe schema deployment workflows.

provides.reasoning: PlanetScale is a fully-managed MySQL-compatible relational database powered by Vitess, providing ACID transactions and SQL query support.
PlanetScale uses Vitess under the hood to provide horizontal sharding and automatic scaling across multiple database shards.
PlanetScale's deploy request workflow enables schema changes without table locks, a unique capability that differentiates it from traditional MySQL deployments.
PlanetScale provides a branching model for databases similar to git branches, allowing safe development and staging workflows for schema changes.
PlanetScale includes built-in connection pooling via PlanetScale Proxy, enabling high concurrency without exhausting database connections.
PlanetScale is a fully-managed serverless database service with automated backups, monitoring, and zero-downtime deployments.

requires.reasoning: As a cloud SaaS database, PlanetScale requires internet connectivity for application connections to the managed database clusters.
PlanetScale is MySQL-compatible and requires any MySQL-compatible driver or client library for connecting from application code.

provider.description: Serverless MySQL-compatible database provider (PlanetScale is the primary option for this pattern)

connection_pooling.description: Enable PlanetScale's built-in connection pooling via PlanetScale Proxy (recommended for serverless and high-concurrency workloads)

schema_migrations.description: deploy-requests: use PlanetScale's safe migration workflow (recommended); direct: apply migrations directly (faster but less safe)

enable_branching.description: Enable database branching for dev/staging workflows, allowing schema changes to be tested in isolated branches before production deployment

boost_mode.description: Enable PlanetScale Boost for in-memory query caching (additional cost, improves read performance for repeated queries)

ssl_mode.description: PlanetScale requires TLS for all connections. 'verify-identity' adds certificate hostname validation.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: PlanetScale removed their free tier in March 2024. The cheapest available plan is Scaler Pro at $39/month (10GB storage, 1 production branch, 1 development branch). Mid-tier usage with more storage, branches, and higher throughput reaches $150-300/month. High-traffic production workloads with multiple production branches, horizontal sharding, PlanetScale Boost caching, and enterprise features can reach $500+/month. The $500 max represents a solid production deployment without enterprise tier pricing. Enterprise workloads with dedicated infrastructure and custom SLAs can exceed thousands per month.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium adoption complexity. Developers familiar with MySQL can connect quickly using existing MySQL drivers. However, PlanetScale has MySQL feature restrictions (no foreign key constraints by default, no full-text search in some configurations) that require schema adjustments. The deploy request workflow for schema changes is new for most teams (1-2 days to learn). Setting up branching strategies for dev/staging adds configuration overhead. Vitess-specific behaviors (e.g., scattered queries across shards) need awareness for performance-sensitive queries. With AI coding assistants, connection setup and query writing is straightforward; the main learning investment is in PlanetScale's migration and branching model.
provenance.licenseCost: PlanetScale operates on a consumption-based SaaS pricing model with no upfront license fees. The underlying Vitess engine is open source (Apache 2.0), but PlanetScale itself is a commercial managed service. No separate database license purchase required; costs are entirely usage-based (storage, rows read/written, branches). The Scaler Pro plan starts at $39/month with no per-seat licensing. Enterprise agreements are available but optional.

supports_nfr.reason: PlanetScale provides up to 99.99% availability with their managed Vitess clusters and automatic failover
PlanetScale serverless MySQL has a minimum p95 latency of ~5ms for simple indexed queries due to connection overhead and network round-trip
At p99, PlanetScale latency accounts for tail latency from Vitess routing overhead and connection pool contention, with a floor of ~20ms
PlanetScale automated failover via Vitess typically completes within 1-2 minutes for primary failover events
PlanetScale with Vitess horizontal sharding supports up to ~500K read QPS across shards with proper schema and indexing
PlanetScale write throughput scales horizontally via Vitess sharding up to ~100K write QPS across a distributed cluster
PlanetScale provides query insights and audit logging capabilities available on higher tiers but optional for all workloads
PlanetScale supports row-level isolation, schema-per-tenant via separate databases/branches, or full per-tenant database isolation patterns
PlanetScale supports PII workloads with encryption at rest and in transit; works for both PII and non-PII data scenarios
PlanetScale offers HIPAA BAA on enterprise plans; also suitable for non-healthcare applications without HIPAA requirements
PlanetScale supports GDPR compliance through data residency options and deletion capabilities; works for both GDPR and non-GDPR use cases
CCPA requirements can be met through standard data access controls and deletion workflows; pattern works for non-CCPA cases as well
SOX compliance supported through audit trails and access controls on appropriate tiers; also applicable to non-financial applications
PlanetScale with Vitess supports read-your-writes consistency when routing reads to the primary; optional for workloads tolerating eventual consistency on replicas
PlanetScale provides strict durability via synchronous replication and automatic backups; also supports relaxed durability configurations for performance

supports_constraints.reason: PlanetScale infrastructure runs on AWS and GCP; accessible from any cloud or on-premises environment with internet connectivity
PlanetScale is MySQL-compatible and any language with a MySQL or compatible driver can connect to it, covering all major languages
PlanetScale is a serverless relational database accessible from any platform type that can establish a MySQL connection over the internet
Pattern specifically requires PlanetScale as the managed MySQL database provider
PlanetScale is specifically designed for OLTP transactional workloads; this pattern is only applicable when OLTP workload is required
PlanetScale is not suitable for OLAP analytical workloads; the pattern does not apply when olap_workload is required
PlanetScale does not provide native vector search, but can coexist with a separate vector database service in the same architecture
PlanetScale is a relational MySQL database and is not applicable when a document-oriented data model is required
PlanetScale works with or without a separate caching layer; PlanetScale Boost provides optional in-memory query caching
PlanetScale works alongside async messaging patterns and does not conflict with message queue architectures
PlanetScale can store AI model metadata, inference results, and structured application data regardless of AI inference requirements
PlanetScale supports multi-tenant architectures through row-level isolation, schema-per-tenant, or per-tenant database patterns
PlanetScale supports batch processing workloads using standard MySQL queries and transactions alongside real-time OLTP workloads
PlanetScale supports ACID transactions within a single shard; cross-shard transactions are possible via Vitess but with limitations
PlanetScale does not provide native change data capture streaming out of the box, but can be paired with external CDC tools
MySQL relational tables can serve simple key-value lookups, but PlanetScale is not a dedicated key-value store
PlanetScale is a relational database and is not applicable when graph database capabilities are required
MySQL can store time-series data with timestamp columns but is not optimized for high-volume time-series workloads; works for moderate time-series needs

warn_nfr.reason: PlanetScale removed their free tier in March 2024. The minimum Scaler Pro plan starts at $39/month. This pattern is not suitable for zero-budget or sub-$39/month cost constraints.
warn_nfr.message: db-managed-mysql--planetscale: monthly cost ceiling is {actual} USD (<$39). PlanetScale's cheapest plan (Scaler Pro) starts at $39/month — no free tier is available. Consider a self-hosted MySQL or a free-tier alternative.
